content.problemStatement:
Katya has a sequence of integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ana_1, a_2, \dots, a_na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,an‚Äã and an integer mmm.

She defines a good sequence of integers as a non-empty sequence such that the sum of the elements in each of its non-empty subsequences is divisible by mmm.

Katya wants to know the number of good subsequences of the sequence aaa. Can you help her?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers nnn and mmm.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ana_1, a_2, \dots, a_na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,an‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of good subsequences.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are no good subsequences.
Example case 2: There is exactly one good subsequence of aaa: [3][3][3].
